category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate an Employee master entity
items.description: Emit an Employee entity: code, name, department, designation, date of joining, status (Active/Left), and links to user and (optionally) party. It is the anchor master for attendance, leave and payroll, and should enforce a unique employee code.
items.applicability: HR module scaffolding; the root master for all people-related records.
items.example: EMP-0001 Jane Doe, dept Finance, joined 2026-01-10, status Active.
items.limitation: This is a master-data skeleton; full HRMS concerns (org charts, contracts, statutory IDs, document expiry) are not included and must not be implied.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate an Attendance skeleton
items.description: Emit an Attendance entity keyed by employee and date with a status (Present/Absent/Half-Day/On-Leave) and optional in/out time. Enforce one record per employee per date. It feeds payroll day-counts but performs no payroll calculation itself.
items.limitation: This is a record-keeping skeleton: shift rules, overtime calculation, biometric integration and leave-balance accrual are out of scope and must be flagged as not implemented.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate a Payroll skeleton (salary structure + slip)
items.description: Emit a Salary Structure (earning and deduction components with amount or formula) and a Salary Slip that, for a period, computes gross = sum(earnings), total deductions, and net pay per employee. On submit it may post a journal: Debit Salary Expense / Credit Payable and statutory accounts.
items.example: Slip EMP-0001 2026-06: Basic 3000 + Allowance 500 - Tax 350 = net 3150; Dr Salary 3500 / Cr Tax Payable 350 / Cr Net Payable 3150.
items.limitation: This is a calculation-and-post skeleton; statutory tax tables, loan recovery, proration and jurisdiction-specific compliance are NOT implemented and must be stated as gaps.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate a POS Sale entry
items.description: Emit a Point-of-Sale sale that is a Sales Invoice with immediate payment. On submit it posts revenue/AR and a paired receipt clearing AR to cash, plus a stock issue for goods. Header: POS profile, customer (optional walk-in), payment mode; lines: item, qty, rate.
items.applicability: Retail/POS module; counter sales with instant settlement.
items.example: POS-0001: 2 x WidgetA @ 100 + 15% tax = 230, paid cash; Dr Cash 230 / Cr Sales 200 / Cr Output Tax 30; stock -2.
items.limitation: This reuses the invoice/payment/stock posting primitives; offline-mode sync, cash-drawer reconciliation and loyalty are separate features not covered here.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate an eCommerce Order ingestion skeleton
items.description: Emit an eCommerce Order entity that maps an external storefront order to internal Sales Order + Sales Invoice + Delivery. Capture external order id, channel, customer, lines and payment status; convert to internal documents idempotently keyed on the external id to avoid duplicate posting.
items.applicability: Channel-integration module; bridging an online store to the ERP.
items.example: ECOM ext-id SHOP-9921 -> SO-0050 -> SINV-0050 -> DN-0050; re-ingesting SHOP-9921 is a no-op.
items.limitation: This is an ingestion skeleton; real connectors (webhooks, inventory sync back to the channel, refunds) are integration work not provided, and idempotency keying must be enforced or duplicates will post.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate a Support Ticket (helpdesk) skeleton
items.description: Emit a Support Ticket entity: subject, customer/contact, priority, status (Open/In-Progress/Resolved/Closed), assigned agent, and a threaded reply log with timestamps. Status transitions are an explicit state machine; SLA timers are optional metadata.
items.example: TKT-0001 'Login fails', customer ACME, priority High, status Open, assigned to agent Bob.
items.limitation: This is a tracking skeleton; automated SLA escalation, email-to-ticket parsing and knowledge-base suggestions are out of scope and must not be implied.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate a Project and Task skeleton
items.description: Emit a Project entity (name, customer, status, start/end) and child Task entities (subject, assignee, status, due date, % complete, optional parent for sub-tasks). Tasks roll up progress to the project. Optional time logs link to tasks for effort capture.
items.applicability: Project-management module; work breakdown and tracking.
items.example: PRJ-0001 'ERP rollout' with TASK-001 'Data migration' assigned to Jane, 40% complete, due 2026-07-15.
items.limitation: This is a tracking skeleton; Gantt scheduling, dependencies, resource levelling and billing-from-timesheets are separate features not included here.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate an Asset register with maintenance link
items.description: Emit a Fixed Asset entity: code, category, purchase date, gross value, and status (In-Use/Disposed). Optionally link to the maintenance schedule and to a depreciation routine. The register is the master that depreciation journals and maintenance records reference.
items.applicability: Fixed-asset module; the master for capitalised items.
items.example: AST-0001 Machine-7, category Plant, purchased 2026-01-10 for 20000, status In-Use.
items.limitation: This is a register skeleton; depreciation methods, revaluation, disposal gain/loss posting and componentisation are separate accounting features not implied by registration alone.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate Custom Fields as additive metadata, never schema edits
items.description: Allow per-entity custom fields through an additive mechanism (extra columns on an extension table or a typed key/value side table) so customers extend entities without altering core tables or breaking migrations. Custom fields render in the form and persist with the record.
items.applicability: Customization framework; the supported way to extend generated entities.
items.example: Add 'Region' custom field to Customer: stored in a customer-extension table, shown on the form, queryable in reports.
items.limitation: Key/value side tables sacrifice type safety and query performance; heavy reporting on custom fields may need promotion to real columns. Custom fields must never trigger a destructive core-schema migration.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate Numbering Series per document type
items.description: Every transactional document must draw its name from a configurable numbering series (prefix + zero-padded counter, optionally with year/period), allocated atomically so no two documents share a number and there are no gaps from concurrent saves.
items.applicability: Document-identity framework shared by all generated transactional entities.
items.example: Series 'SINV-.YYYY.-' yields SINV-2026-00001, SINV-2026-00002; allocation is transactional to prevent duplicates.
items.limitation: Gapless atomic allocation can serialise inserts under high concurrency; if gaps are acceptable, a cheaper sequence may be used, but legal numbering requirements (e.g. tax invoices) often forbid gaps and must be confirmed.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate an Import (CSV/Excel) skeleton with validation
items.description: Emit a generic import that maps spreadsheet columns to entity fields, validates each row (types, required, referential lookups), reports per-row errors, and commits only valid rows (or none, per all-or-nothing policy). Imports of posting documents must route through the same validation/posting service as manual entry.
items.applicability: Data-migration and bulk-entry tooling for any generated entity.
items.example: Import 500 customers from CSV: 3 rows fail (missing AR account) and are reported; 497 commit; the 3 are re-uploaded after fixing.
items.limitation: Importing posting documents that bypass the posting service is dangerous — it can create unbalanced or unvalidated ledger rows. Imports must reuse domain validation, not write directly to tables.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate an Export skeleton for any list view
items.description: Provide export to CSV/Excel from any list/report, honouring the current filters and only the columns the user can see. Export must page through results server-side rather than materialising large text columns, to avoid loading full content for every row.
items.applicability: Reporting/data-IO tooling across all list views.
items.example: Export filtered Sales Invoices (status Submitted, May) to CSV with columns name, customer, total, outstanding.
items.limitation: Exports of large datasets must stream/paginate; naive full-table loads of heavy text columns (content/raw-text) cause memory and timeout issues, mirroring the project's list-query rules.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate Leave Application + balance skeleton
items.description: Emit a Leave Application (employee, leave type, from/to dates, days, status) governed by a maker-checker approval, and a simple leave-balance ledger that debits approved leave against an allocated balance per type.
items.applicability: HR module; absence request and approval.
items.example: LV-0001 EMP-0001 Annual 2026-07-01..07-03 = 3 days, approved; Annual balance 12 -> 9.
items.limitation: This is an approval-and-ledger skeleton; carry-forward rules, encashment, accrual schedules and statutory entitlements are jurisdiction-specific and NOT implemented.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate a POS Profile / closing voucher skeleton
items.description: Emit a POS Profile (default warehouse, customer, payment modes, price list) that seeds POS sales, and a POS Closing entry that totals the period's sales and tendered amounts per payment mode for cash-up. Closing reconciles expected vs counted cash.
items.applicability: Retail module; shift configuration and end-of-day reconciliation.
items.example: Profile 'Counter-1' WH-Store, cash+card; closing for 2026-06-01 shows expected cash 1500 vs counted 1480, variance -20.
items.limitation: Variance is recorded, not auto-resolved; cash-drawer fraud controls, blind counts and multi-currency tendering are out of scope for this skeleton.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate a Contact / Address book shared across parties
items.description: Emit reusable Contact and Address entities linkable to customers, suppliers and employees via a polymorphic link, so the same person/location is not duplicated per party. Capture name, email, phone for contacts; line, city, country for addresses.
items.applicability: Shared master-data scaffolding for CRM, sales and purchasing.
items.example: Contact 'John (ACME)' linked to Customer ACME and an address 'HQ, London' reused on quotations and invoices.
items.limitation: A polymorphic link needs careful referential-integrity handling; orphaned contacts on party deletion and duplicate detection (same email) are concerns this skeleton flags but does not fully solve.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate a Lead -> Opportunity CRM skeleton
items.description: Emit a Lead (unqualified interest) and Opportunity (qualified deal with stage, value, expected close) with a state machine Lead -> Opportunity -> Quotation, so the sales pipeline connects into the order-to-cash flow already generated.
items.applicability: CRM module; top-of-funnel sales tracking feeding quotations.
items.example: LEAD-0001 web enquiry -> OPP-0001 'ACME deal' stage Proposal value 10000 -> QTN-0001.
items.limitation: This is a pipeline skeleton; lead scoring, campaign attribution and forecasting analytics are out of scope and the stage list is a starting default, not a fixed methodology.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate user-defined Print Formats / templates safely
items.description: Allow document print formats to be customer-configurable templates bound to entity fields, rendered server-side. Templates must read only authorised fields for the current user and must escape output to prevent injection; they must not execute arbitrary code.
items.applicability: Output/customization framework for invoices, orders and reports.
items.example: Custom invoice template injects {customer}, {grand_total}, {tax_breakup}; rendered PDF respects field-level access.
items.limitation: Template engines are an injection and data-leak surface; allowing raw code or unrestricted field access in templates is a security risk that must be sandboxed, not trusted to the template author.

items.category: ERP HR / POS / eCommerce / Customization Generation
items.title: Generate a Timesheet skeleton linked to tasks
items.description: Emit a Timesheet entity capturing employee, date, task/project reference, hours and an optional billable flag, rolling hours up to the linked task and project. It records effort for capacity and (optionally) billing, but does not itself post payroll or invoices.
items.applicability: Project/HR module; effort capture against work.
items.example: TS-0001 EMP-0001 2026-06-02 4h on TASK-001 (billable); TASK-001 logged effort += 4h.
items.limitation: This is an effort-capture skeleton; approval of timesheets, billing-rate resolution, overtime and conversion to a sales invoice are separate features not included here.
